name: Dawson, Francis
description: This chap is better known as "Slim," And when it comes to ladies' men, He is always in the swim.

name: Jackson, Carrie
motto: "And yet she is like an angel fair, With rosy cheeks and light hair, And like a lady of leisure, Who is strolling out for pleasure."

name: Upchurch, Aubrey
clubs: Guardian of second base, the "Aggie" baseball team
description: He has a sober, dignified appearance which is likely to deceive.

name: Mendenhall, Mildred
motto: "Brown eyes are the eyes for me."
description: At all hours of the day Mildred's joyous laugh may be heard. She enjoys the days for birds, for she is fond of catching a "Jay Bird."

name: Pearce, Joe
description: Joe is a good natured lad who thinks that passing in his studies is of as much importance as acquiring fame in athletics.

name: Huddleston, Leslie
description: With a thoughtful, calm, and quiet grace, "Mollie" is never out of his place. When Judge Alden lectured on "Choosing a Wife" "Mollie" took notes, you bet your life.

name: Ward, Essie
motto: "And welcome wheresoever she went, A calm and gracious element."
description: She came to us as a graduate from the High School at Magnolia, and we are glad that she has cast her lot with us, because she inspires her friends with awe and wonder, as a Junior should.

name: Webb, Barbara
motto: "Oh! she is there, I heard her giggle."
description: Barbara is a jolly girl, however she is not brave, for if you just say "lizard" she is sure to run.

name: Cleaver, Sula Mae
motto: "Her hair is dark, and dark her starry eyes, And she is hard to beat in the science of making pies."

name: Green, Chester
description: He is a star football player. Chester is a very jolly boy and probably has more friends among the girls than any other boy.

name: Sanders, Freddie
description: She is so quiet sometimes that you would not expect such depth of fun in her. She is always faithful to her studies and is beloved of her teachers.

name: Stavely, Floyd
description: Floyd gives the teachers very little trouble, as he always sleeps through most of his classes. However, in spite of his drowsiness he manages to get good grades.

name: Sanders, Eula
description: Eula may appear to be silent at times. But if anyone wishes to find out whether she ever talks, let him come down to the Chemistry laboratory. We understand that Eula is devoted to science.

name: Logan, Mendenhall
description: Logan is one of those serious-minded young fellows who has the idea that studying is the most important part of going to school. He attends strictly to his own business without saying very much about it.

name: Floye, Hudson
motto: "I love Holice, I love Floyd, The love for these boys I cannot avoid. Boys, God bless you, I love you both Because you both love me."

name: Jim, Blair
clubs: Assistant Business Manager and Agricultural Editor of the Monitor staff
description: He is quiet, but one of the few who actually studies his lessons.

name: Fannie, Lee May
motto: "How sweetly Fannie Lee sweeps along, Her step is music and her voice is song."
description: She is a very studious child and one of Mr. Strickland's prime favorites.

name: Conrad, Lewis
description: Well liked by everyone, especially the girls. Nothing pleases him better than to put on a three-inch collar and go strolling out to Pittman's pond with Verda. He realizes that he makes a great impression on these occasions.

name: Maylene, Russ
description: Maylene is a modest little lassie. She has two characteristics, seriousness and dignity.

name: Walter, Ewing
description: Blue eyes and light hair. He is a chap that is exceedingly rare, And as to the picture show, he is always there.

name: Andy, Anderson
motto: "A rosy boy, right plump to see, A rollicking, toddling little lad."
description: He is active in all lines of football, etc. He has a good nature and is friendly to all.

name: Webb, Carl
description: Carl was a winner in the Magnolia and Monticello debate.

name: Austin, Floy May
motto: "Her thoughts she speaks in musical tones."
description: Floy is one of our best students, and can be relied upon to do her part in every field of work. She won honors for our school in the piano contest at Pine Bluff in May.
